content.text: Jetpack Compose's reactive model is a natural match for streamed AI output: the UI recomposes automatically whenever state changes, so each incoming token from Claude just needs to update a
and Compose handles the rest. The challenge is wiring the

from the network layer into stable, lifecycle-safe Compose state — choosing the right collection point, preventing recomposition storms, auto-scrolling as text grows, and showing a blinking cursor while the stream is active.

attrs.alt: Streaming state machine: Flow source → ViewModel StateFlow → Compose collectAsStateWithLifecycle → rendered output; Idle → Streaming → Accumulating → Complete state transitions
attrs.title: Collecting Streamed Tokens in Jetpack Compose Architecture

content.text: Keep streaming state separate from the completed message list. Two
s cover everything the UI needs: the accumulating text (updated on every token) and a boolean flag that drives the loading indicator and the Stop button:

changes, which avoids re-measuring every historical message on every token.
. The lifecycle-aware variant pauses collection when the app goes to the background, preventing wasted recompositions and potential memory leaks. It requires the
A chat UI should scroll to the bottom as the assistant's response grows. Use
keyed on the total item count. The effect fires once per new message, not on every token — scroll-to-bottom happens when a completed assistant message is appended:
) means we scroll once when the assistant bubble appears and once when it completes — not hundreds of times per second as tokens arrive. Token-level scroll would cause visible jitter.
A blinking cursor next to the growing text signals that more tokens are coming.
drives a float from 1 to 0 and back, which the cursor
uses as its alpha. Only allocate the transition when
is true to avoid consuming animation resources in the resting state:
While streaming, replace the Send button with a Stop button that calls
. This cancels the coroutine collecting the Flow, which causes the upstream SSE connection to close via
Keeps collecting; recomposes even when screen is hidden
Simple previews or state that must stay live when backgrounded
Pauses at STARTED → CREATED, resumes on return to foreground
All production screens — the default choice for chat UI
Updating StateFlow off the main thread.
fires on OkHttp's thread pool. If you emit to a
there, Compose reads are safe (StateFlow is thread-safe), but double-check any mutable list or string builder you accumulate outside the ViewModel.
Using integer indices as item keys causes mismatches when messages are appended. Use a stable unique key (message ID, UUID, or content hash). The in-progress
must be unique and stable across the streaming lifecycle.
on every token floods the Compose animation system. Debounce or key the LaunchedEffect on message count, not character count.

event, so the UI must clean up explicitly.
